channel.title: What Is Being Built — Daily Activity Feed
channel.description: Daily updates on open-source automated research tools: commits, releases, and community activity.

item.title: Daily digest — 2026-07-24
item.description:
Our open-source research community has seen some exciting updates recently. The `scienceverse/metacheck` project has added a new `reproducibility_check` feature to its `data_check` branch, courtesy of Lakens, which will likely enhance the tool's ability to ensure reliable research outcomes. Meanwhile, the `marton-balazs-kovacs/tenzing` project has released version 0.4.1, with Anka building the site for this new version and Alex O. Holcombe updating the success alert title in the `sweet_alert.js` file. These updates demonstrate the community's ongoing commitment to improving research tools and methodologies. As our projects continue to evolve, we can expect to see even more innovative features and collaborations emerge, ultimately strengthening the foundation of open-source research. With these latest developments, we're one step closer to fostering a more transparent and reproducible research environment.

17 commits across 4 active repositories this week.

New releases: oddpub v7.3.0

Most active:

tenzing9 commits
metacheck6 commits
oddpub1 commits
escicheck1 commits
